mission.title: dragonfly: Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull
mission.description: Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull . This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify dragonfly troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your dragonfly deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly -n dragonfly-system --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review dragonfly configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dragonfly configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
kubectl get configmap -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dragonfly
```
See this https://github.com/BlackYuzia/Dragonfly-Bull to reproduce.

1. Dragonfly rejects EVAL under MULTI (which could potentially be fixed by making the multi TX global - dynamically)
2.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull
steps.description:
Removes `OpRem` and `OpSet` assertions that check the new element is not empty. Running locally both work fine with empty elements.

Empty elements in `LREM` and `LSET` are supported by Redis, eg `EVAL "redis.call('RPUSH', 'mylist', 'one'); redis.call('LSET', 'mylist', 0, '');" 0`, and are used by bullmq (https://github.com/taskforcesh/bullmq/blob/master/src/commands/includes/cleanList.lua#L36) (see https://github.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/issues/782)

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull  is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly -n dragonfly-system --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n dragonfly-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Removes `OpRem` and `OpSet` assertions that check the new element is not empty. Running locally both work fine with empty elements.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dragonfly installed or the issue environment reproducible.
